So I grew up in New Jersey.

helping different people during natural disasters, wild and forest firefighters,

and I became a wildland forest firefighter.

i had uh been on a um 17 day camping trip i had a bunch of vacation time saved up uh from working

I thought I was with the girl that I was going to marry.

And one evening I was having a moment. I was just feeling the feelings. And I was here alone at my parents' house. And I decided to go on a walk into the forest at night without a flashlight because this is where I grew up.

The forest out here is my serenity. I go into the forest to find peace. But at this moment in time, I wasn't in the best headspace. And I went into the forest right across the street from my house, this 36,000 acre preserve, which has a recorded Bigfoot sighting. But that was not my concern at the moment.

I just wanted to go out into the forest. There's a spot out there called Devil's Half Acre. And it's basically, it's an unknown spot. Like you could look up Devil's Half Acre in the Sourlands. I'm sure you'll find something, but there's not too many people that know where it is. I think I might be the one person who actually knows the location today, who lives in Hillsboro. But there's no trails that take you there. It's off the beaten path.
So anyway, it's not a far walk from my parents' house.
It's basically a 10-minute walk through the forest.

The more you hike at night, the better your night vision gets.
And I'm always trying to practice that.
So I went out into the forest at night with a broken heart, really mourning this whole relationship, not really exactly knowing how to express my emotions and stuff.

and just wasn't the typical place that you'd hear a canine of that size.
